original_text:
### Bolding Rules **[GENERAL — calibrated to the guide's callout/chart patterns]**
- Bold is for **hierarchy and key takeaways, not decoration**. Per email section, bold at most: the heading(s), one key phrase or claim per paragraph, and the statistical callout figure.
- Always bold: callout text per the callout specs (§1.7), statistical callout numbers, brand name within a benefit claim when it is the focal point of the sentence (first instance per section only).
- Never bold: entire paragraphs, footnotes/references, more than ~15% of running body text in any section, prepositional filler, the generic name.
- Never use bold + ALL CAPS + color emphasis simultaneously on the same string except in chart headlines and the campaign headline lockup. Pick at most two emphasis devices per element.
- No underlining except real hyperlinks. No italic for emphasis in patient copy — reserve italics for study names, notes ("Note:"), and citations.

relations.note: Caps the number/combination of emphasis devices layered onto the bold usage already governed by the hierarchy rule.
Both rules jointly define the closed set of permitted emphasis devices (bold, caps, color, underline, italic) and their exclusivity conditions.

quotes: more than ~15% of running body text in any section
Bold is for **hierarchy and key takeaways, not decoration**.
Always bold: callout text per the callout specs (§1.7), statistical callout numbers, brand name within a benefit claim when it is the focal point of the sentence (first instance per section only).
Never bold: entire paragraphs, footnotes/references, more than ~15% of running body text in any section, prepositional filler, the generic name.

rules.intent: Keep bold emphasis purposeful and legible rather than decorative or overused.
rules.rule_text: Bold is reserved for hierarchy and key takeaways, not decoration. Per email section, bold at most: the heading(s), one key phrase/claim per paragraph, and the statistical callout figure. Always bold callout text per callout specs, statistical callout numbers, and the brand name within a benefit claim when it is the sentence's focal point (first instance per section only). Never bold entire paragraphs, footnotes/references, prepositional filler, the generic name, or more than ~15% of running body text in any section.

effect.reserved_for: chart headlines and the campaign headline lockup
effect.subject: bold + ALL CAPS + color emphasis combined on the same string

quotes: Never use bold + ALL CAPS + color emphasis simultaneously on the same string except in chart headlines and the campaign headline lockup.
Never use bold + ALL CAPS + color emphasis simultaneously on the same string except in chart headlines and the campaign headline lockup.
Pick at most two emphasis devices per element.

rules.intent: Prevent visual over-emphasis and preserve the chart-headline/campaign-lockup as the only sanctioned maximal-emphasis device.
rules.rule_text: Never combine bold + ALL CAPS + color emphasis simultaneously on the same string, except in chart headlines and the campaign headline lockup, which are the sole devices permitted this triple-stack treatment. Elsewhere, pick at most two emphasis devices per element.

quotes: No italic for emphasis in patient copy — reserve italics for study names, notes ("Note:"), and citations.

rules.intent: Keep underline and italic reserved for their functional/editorial roles so they remain unambiguous signals.
rules.rule_text: Underlining is reserved exclusively for real hyperlinks and must not be used elsewhere. Italics must not be used for emphasis in patient copy; reserve italics for study names, notes (e.g., "Note:"), and citations.
